The implementation requires careful handling of JavaScript Maps for potentially negative keys.

```javascript
/**
 * @param {string} s
 * @return {number}
 */
function solve(s) {
    const N = s.length;
    
    // Map to store the minimum index 'l' encountered for a specific prefix sum balance B.
    // Key: Balance B, Value: Smallest index l such that P[l] = B.
    const minIndex = new Map();
    
    // P[0] = 0, corresponding to the empty prefix ending at index 0.
    minIndex.set(0, 0);
    
    let currentBalance = 0;
    let maxLen = 0;

    for (let j = 1; j <= N; j++) {
        // Calculate P[j] based on s[j-1]
        const char = s[j - 1];
        // Map '1' -> +1, '0' -> -1
        currentBalance += (char === '1' ? 1 : -1);

        // We look for an index l < j such that P[j] - P[l] = D, where D is 0, 2, or -2.
        // L = j - l. We maximize L by minimizing l.

        // Case D = 0: P[l] = P[j]
        if (minIndex.has(currentBalance)) {
            const l = minIndex.get(currentBalance);
            maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, j - l);
        }

        // Case D = 2: P[l] = P[j] - 2. (Original segment needs +2 balance)
        // This corresponds to the required balance shift D = -2 to achieve 0.
        const targetB_D2 = currentBalance - 2;
        if (minIndex.has(targetB_D2)) {
            const l = minIndex.get(targetB_D2);
            maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, j - l);
        }

        // Case D = -2: P[l] = P[j] + 2. (Original segment needs -2 balance)
        // This corresponds to the required balance shift D = 2 to achieve 0.
        const targetB_D_minus_2 = currentBalance + 2;
        if (minIndex.has(targetB_D_minus_2)) {
            const l = minIndex.get(targetB_D_minus_2);
            maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, j - l);
        }

        // Update minIndex only if this balance hasn't been seen before.
        if (!minIndex.has(currentBalance)) {
            minIndex.set(currentBalance, j);
        }
    }

    return maxLen;
}
